texto: There is no miserable place waiting for you, no hell realm, sitting and waiting like Alaska—waiting to turn you into ice cream. But whatever you call it—hell or the suffering realms—it is something that you enter by creating a world of neurotic fantasy and believing it to be real. It sounds simple, but that's exactly what happens.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lama Thubten Yeshe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “There is no miserable place waiting for you, no hell realm, sitting and waiting like Alaska—waiti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, buddhism, hell pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de hell e hell-on-earth.
